query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "self-improving language models with bidirectional evolutionary search" linguistics
rationale: This paper addresses the core mechanism of "LLM self-improvement" and the specific phenomenon of performance plateaus and distributional bias (tail narrowing) that the user's referenced work ("Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search") investigates, satisfying criteria (a) for same-mechanism evidence and (b) for measuring the dependent variable (self-improvement efficacy) in the same domain.
